title: PHI Computer Services Professional Services Division: Brochure with Kastner Profile, Memoir of Junior Programmer Era, and PHI Engagements (1969-1972)
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Combines a primary-source PHI Update brochure (~1971-1972) describing PHI's Professional Services Division (PSD) — including Kastner's profile as a Systems Analyst in the Financial Systems Group — with Kastner's personal memoir of his March 1969 entry into PHI as a junior programmer at $7,200/year, the 1960s-era apprenticeship learning model, and his work on Marine Midland bond-portfolio Y2K-precursor changes, the First Chicago correspondent-bank DDA system, the Cumberland Farms gas-station accounting system, and the optimization of PHI's internal accounting system.
importance_rationale: Combines rare published primary-source corporate brochure documenting the Wang-Laboratories-owned PHI subsidiary's 1971-72 capabilities with Kastner's first-person career-genesis memoir.
relevance_rationale: PHI's Telecommunications Programming System (TPS) and IBM 360 channel-interface laboratory work remain relevant to telecom-controller and IBM-compatible hardware history; the apprenticeship learning model contrasts informatively with modern software development.
prescience_rationale: Memoir / employer-record content; no forward predictions made.
